Step 7 - Gradually pour in warm milk while whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Step 8 - Continue whisking until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon (about 5-7 minutes). Step 9 - Reduce heat to low. Add salt, black pepper, paprika, and nutmeg (if using). Step 10 - Add the grated cheddar and Gruyčre cheeses one handful at a time, stirring until completely melted before adding more. Step 11 - Once all cheese is melted and sauce is smooth, remove from heat. Step 12 - Add the cooked macaroni to the cheese sauce. Stir gently until every piece is coated. Step 13 - Pour the mac and cheese into the prepared baking dish and spread evenly. Step 14 - Make the topping: In a small bowl, mix breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan, and melted butter until combined. Step 15 - Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture evenly over the mac and cheese. Step 16 - Bake for 20-25 minutes until bubbly and the top is golden brown and crispy. Step 17 - Let it rest for 5-10 minutes before serving (the sauce will set slightly).
serving_suggestion: Serve hot as a main dish or side dish. Pairs well with fried chicken, barbecue ribs, or a simple green salad.
alternative_cooking: Add cooked bacon, lobster, truffle oil, or caramelized onions for variations. For stovetop mac and cheese (not baked), skip the breadcrumb topping and serve immediately after mixing.

name: Hamburger
description: Classic American hamburger with a juicy beef patty, melted cheese, fresh vegetables, and signature sauce in a soft bun.
ingredients: 500g ground beef (80/20 lean to fat ratio for juiciness)|4 hamburger buns (brioche or potato buns)|4 slices American or cheddar cheese|1 large tomato (sliced)|1 onion (thinly sliced into rings)|4 leaves lettuce (iceberg or romaine)|8 pickles (sliced)|2 tablespoons butter (for toasting buns)|For seasoning: 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 1 teaspoon garlic powder|For sauce: 4 tablespoons mayonnaise, 2 tablespoons ketchup, 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 tablespoon sweet pickle relish, 1 teaspoon white vinegar, 1/2 teaspoon paprika
instructions: Step 1 - Make the patties: Divide ground beef into 4 equal portions (about 125g each). Gently shape each portion into a ball, then flatten into a patty about 2cm thick. Make a small indentation in the center with your thumb (this prevents the patty from bulging during cooking). Step 2 - Season both sides of each patty generously with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Step 3 - Refrigerate patties for 15-30 minutes while preparing other ingredients. Step 4 - Make the special sauce: In a small bowl, mix mayonnaise, ketchup, mustard, sweet relish, white vinegar, and paprika. Stir until well combined. Refrigerate until ready to use. Step 5 - Prepare all toppings: Slice tomato, onion rings, lettuce leaves, and pickles. Set aside. Step 6 - Heat a large cast iron skillet, grill pan, or outdoor grill over medium-high heat. Step 7 - Cook the patties: Place patties on the hot surface. Do not press down with a spatula (this releases juices). Step 8 - Cook for 3-4 minutes until a brown crust forms. Flip once (only once). Step 9 - Cook for another 3-4 minutes for medium. Adjust time for desired doneness: 2 minutes for rare, 4 minutes for medium-well. Step 10 - Add cheese slice to each patty during the last minute of cooking. Cover the pan or grill to help the cheese melt. Step 11 - While patties are cooking, toast the buns: Spread butter on the cut sides of each bun. Place cut-side down on a separate skillet or grill for 1-2 minutes until golden brown. Step 12 - Assemble the burgers: Spread special sauce on the bottom bun. Step 13 - Add lettuce leaf, then the cheeseburger patty. Step 14 - Top with tomato slices, onion rings, and pickles. Step 15 - Spread more sauce on the top bun (optional). Step 16 - Place the top bun on the burger. Step 17 - Press down gently and serve immediately.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with french fries, onion rings, coleslaw, or a dill pickle spear on the side. A cold soda or milkshake completes the meal.
alternative_cooking: For cheeseburger variations: add crispy bacon (bacon cheeseburger), fried egg, jalapeńos for heat, or mushrooms and Swiss cheese. For a healthier version, use ground turkey or a plant-based patty.

name: Pizza Margherita
description: Classic Neapolitan pizza with simple, high-quality toppings: tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, basil leaves, and olive oil.
ingredients: For the dough: 500g strong bread flour (00 flour is best), 325ml warm water (about 30°C), 10g salt, 5g active dry yeast, 1 tablespoon olive oil|For the toppings: 200g San Marzano tomatoes (crushed) or good quality tomato sauce, 200g fresh mozzarella cheese (buffalo or fior di latte, torn into pieces), Fresh basil leaves (about 12-15 leaves), 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, Salt to taste, Grated Parmesan cheese (optional)
instructions: Step 1 - Make the dough: In a small bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water with a pinch of sugar (optional, to activate). Let sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. Step 2 - In a large bowl or stand mixer with a dough hook, combine flour and salt. Step 3 - Add the yeast mixture and olive oil. Mix on low speed until dough comes together, about 3-5 minutes. Step 4 - Increase speed to medium and knead for 8-10 minutes until dough is smooth, elastic, and slightly sticky. If kneading by hand, knead for 12-15 minutes. Step 5 - Shape dough into a ball. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth or plastic wrap. Step 6 - Let rise at room temperature for 1.5 to 2 hours until doubled in size. Step 7 - Punch down the dough and divide into 2 equal portions (for 2 pizzas). Shape each into a ball. Step 8 - Place dough balls on a floured baking sheet, cover, and let rise for another 30-60 minutes. Step 9 - While dough rises, prepare the toppings: Crush San Marzano tomatoes by hand or with a fork. Add a pinch of salt. Do not cook the sauce. Step 10 - Tear fresh mozzarella into small pieces. Place on a paper towel to drain excess moisture. Step 11 - Place a pizza stone or overturned baking sheet in the oven. Preheat oven to the highest temperature possible (250°C / 480°F or higher) for at least 45 minutes. Step 12 - Stretch the dough: On a floured surface, gently press one dough ball with fingertips to flatten. Step 13 - Lift the dough and gently stretch it over your knuckles, rotating, until it forms a 30-33cm (12-13 inch) circle. Do not use a rolling pin — this crushes air bubbles. Step 14 - Transfer stretched dough to a piece of parchment paper or a lightly floured pizza peel. Step 15 - Top the pizza: Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over the dough, leaving a 2cm border for the crust. Step 16 - Scatter torn mozzarella pieces over the sauce. Step 17 - Drizzle with a little olive oil. Step 18 - Quickly transfer the pizza onto the hot pizza stone in the oven. Step 19 - Bake for 7-10 minutes until the crust is puffed, golden brown, and slightly charred in spots, and the cheese is bubbly and melted. Step 20 - Remove pizza from the oven. Step 21 - Immediately scatter fresh basil leaves over the hot pizza. Step 22 - Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with a little salt. Add grated Parmesan if desired. Step 23 - Slice and serve immediately.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot as a main course with a simple green salad. Pizza Margherita is best eaten fresh, not reheated.
alternative_cooking: For a pizza Margherita, less is more. Do not overload with toppings. Use the highest quality ingredients you can find, especially tomatoes and mozzarella. If you don't have a pizza stone, use an overturned baking sheet preheated in the oven.

name: Pancakes
description: Fluffy, golden breakfast pancakes served with butter and maple syrup — a classic American morning favorite.
ingredients: 200g all-purpose flour|2 tablespoons sugar|1 tablespoon baking powder|1/2 teaspoon salt|1 large egg|300ml milk|2 tablespoons melted butter (plus extra for cooking)|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|For serving: Butter|Maple syrup|Fresh berries (optional)
instructions: Step 1 - In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt until well combined. Step 2 - In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract until smooth. Step 3 - Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Stir gently with a spatula or wooden spoon until just combined. The batter should be slightly lumpy. Do not overmix — overmixing makes pancakes tough. Step 4 - Let the batter rest for 5-10 minutes while you heat the pan. This allows the baking powder to activate. Step 5 - Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Step 6 - Add a small amount of butter to the pan and swirl to coat the surface. Step 7 - Pour 1/4 cup (about 60ml) of batter onto the hot skillet for each pancake. Step 8 - Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges look set (about 2-3 minutes). Step 9 - Flip the pancake using a spatula. The cooked side should be golden brown. Step 10 - Cook for another 1-2 minutes until the second side is golden brown and the pancake is cooked through. Step 11 - Transfer to a plate and keep warm. Repeat with remaining batter, adding more butter to the pan as needed. Step 12 - Serve stacked on a plate. Place a pat of butter on top of the stack. Step 13 - Pour maple syrup generously over the pancakes. Add fresh berries if desired.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot for breakfast or brunch with crispy bacon, scrambled eggs, or fresh fruit on the side.
alternative_cooking: For buttermilk pancakes, replace milk with 300ml buttermilk and reduce baking powder to 1 teaspoon (add 1/2 teaspoon baking soda). For chocolate chip pancakes, sprinkle chocolate chips onto the batter after pouring into the pan.

name: Waffles
description: Crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside — classic waffles made in a waffle iron, perfect for breakfast or dessert.
ingredients: 200g all-purpose flour|2 tablespoons sugar|1 tablespoon baking powder|1/2 teaspoon salt|2 large eggs (separated into yolks and whites)|300ml milk|100g melted butter|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|For serving: Butter|Maple syrup|Whipped cream|Fresh berries|Powdered sugar
instructions: Step 1 - Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions. Step 2 - In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Step 3 - In a separate bowl, whisk the egg yolks, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract until smooth. Step 4 - Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined. The batter will be thick. Step 5 - In a clean, dry bowl, beat the egg whites with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form (about 2-3 minutes). Step 6 - Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter using a spatula. Do not overmix — a few white streaks are fine. This step makes waffles light and fluffy. Step 7 - Let the batter rest for 5 minutes. Step 8 - Lightly grease the waffle iron with cooking spray or a little butter (unless non-stick). Step 9 - Pour enough batter onto the center of the waffle iron to just cover the grid (about 1/2 to 3/4 cup depending on your iron). Step 10 - Close the lid and cook according to the manufacturer's instructions (usually 3-5 minutes) until golden brown and crisp. Step 11 - Carefully remove the waffle using a fork or silicone tongs. Step 12 - Repeat with remaining batter. Step 13 - Serve immediately — waffles are best hot and crisp. Step 14 - Top with a pat of butter, maple syrup, whipped cream, fresh berries, and a dusting of powdered sugar.
serving_suggestion: Serve for breakfast, brunch, or dessert. Pairs with fried chicken (chicken and waffles), bacon, or fresh fruit.
alternative_cooking: For savory waffles, reduce sugar to 1 tablespoon and add 1/2 cup shredded cheese and chopped chives. For chocolate waffles, add 2 tablespoons cocoa powder to the dry ingredients.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
description: Crispy, buttery toasted bread with warm, melted cheese inside — the ultimate quick comfort food.
ingredients: 8 slices bread (white, sourdough, or Texas toast)|4 tablespoons butter (softened)|200g cheese (cheddar, American, mozzarella, or a mix)|Optional additions: Sliced tomato, cooked bacon, ham, avocado, or caramelized onions
instructions: Step 1 - Soften butter at room temperature or microwave for 10 seconds. Step 2 - Spread butter generously on one side of each slice of bread. The buttered side will be the outside that touches the pan. Step 3 - Grate or slice the cheese thinly for even melting. Step 4 - Place 4 slices of bread on a cutting board, buttered side down. Step 5 - Divide the cheese evenly among the 4 slices of bread, spreading to the edges. Step 6 - Add any optional ingredients now (tomato slices, bacon, etc.) if desired. Step 7 - Top with the remaining 4 slices of bread, buttered side up. Step 8 - Heat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-low heat. (Medium-low is important — too high and the bread burns before the cheese melts.) Step 9 - Place sandwiches in the skillet (cook in batches if needed). Step 10 - Cook for 3-4 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Step 11 - Flip carefully using a spatula. Step 12 - Cook for another 3-4 minutes until the second side is golden brown and the cheese is completely melted and gooey. Step 13 - Remove from the pan. Let rest for 1 minute before slicing. Step 14 - Cut each sandwich in half diagonally or into rectangles.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with tomato soup (classic pairing), a side salad, or pickles. Perfect for lunch or a quick dinner.
alternative_cooking: For extra crispy sandwiches, spread mayonnaise instead of butter on the outside of the bread — it creates an amazing golden crust. Use a panini press or waffle iron for a different texture.

name: Shepherd's Pie
description: Classic British comfort food of seasoned ground lamb (or beef) and vegetables topped with creamy mashed potatoes, baked until golden.
ingredients: For the filling: 500g ground lamb (traditional) or ground beef|2 tablespoons olive oil|1 onion (finely chopped)|2 carrots (diced)|2 celery stalks (diced)|3 cloves garlic (minced)|2 tablespoons tomato paste|1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce|250ml beef or lamb broth|100ml red wine (optional)|1 cup frozen peas|1 cup frozen corn|1 teaspoon fresh thyme (or 1/2 teaspoon dried)|Salt and black pepper to taste|For the topping: 1 kg potatoes (Russet or Yukon Gold), peeled and cut into chunks|100ml milk|4 tablespoons butter|1 egg yolk (optional, for richer topping)|1/2 cup grated cheddar cheese (optional)|Salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Step 1 - Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Step 2 - Make the mashed potato topping: Place peeled potato chunks in a large pot and cover with cold salted water. Step 3 - Bring to a boil and cook for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are fork-tender. Step 4 - Drain potatoes well. Return to the pot over low heat for 1 minute to dry out excess moisture. Step 5 - Add butter and milk to the potatoes. Mash until smooth and creamy. Step 6 - Stir in egg yolk (if using) and season with salt and pepper. Set aside. Step 7 - Make the filling: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Step 8 - Add ground lamb (or beef) and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon (about 8-10 minutes). Step 9 - Remove meat with a slotted spoon and set aside. Leave about 2 tablespoons of fat in the pan. Step 10 - Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 5-7 minutes until softened. Step 11 - Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Step 12 - Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes until darkened. Step 13 - Return the meat to the pan. Add Worcestershire sauce, broth, red wine (if using), thyme, salt, and pepper. Step 14 - Bring to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes until the liquid has reduced and thickened. Step 15 - Stir in frozen peas and corn. Cook for 2 more minutes. Step 16 - Transfer the filling to a baking dish (if not using an oven-safe skillet). Spread evenly. Step 17 - Spoon the mashed potatoes over the filling. Spread gently with a fork. Step 18 - Create ridges on the potato surface with a fork — these will become crispy. Step 19 - Sprinkle grated cheddar cheese on top (optional). Step 20 - Place the dish on a baking sheet (to catch any drips). Step 21 - Bake for 20-25 minutes until the filling is bubbly and the potato topping is golden brown. Step 22 - If the top isn't browned enough, broil for 2-3 minutes. Step 23 - Let rest for 10-15 minutes before serving (this allows the filling to set).
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with crusty bread, peas, or a side salad. Leftovers reheat beautifully.
alternative_cooking: Cottage cheese pie uses ground beef instead of lamb. Add 1/2 cup of grated Parmesan to the mashed potatoes for extra flavor. For a healthier topping, use mashed cauliflower instead of potatoes.

name: Chicken Alfredo
description: Creamy, rich pasta dish with tender chicken breast in a Parmesan cream sauce over fettuccine.
ingredients: 400g fettuccine pasta|500g chicken breast (sliced into strips or bite-sized pieces)|4 tablespoons butter|4 cloves garlic (minced)|500ml heavy cream (or double cream)|150g Parmesan cheese (freshly grated, not pre-shredded)|1 teaspoon salt|1/2 teaspoon black pepper|1/4 teaspoon nutmeg (optional)|2 tablespoons olive oil|Fresh parsley for garnish|Extra Parmesan for serving
instructions: Step 1 - Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Step 2 - Cook fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente. Step 3 - Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining. Step 4 - Drain the pasta and set aside. Do not rinse. Step 5 - While pasta is cooking, season chicken strips with salt and pepper on both sides. Step 6 - Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Step 7 - Add chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature 74°C / 165°F). Step 8 - Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside on a plate. Step 9 - Reduce heat to medium. In the same skillet, melt butter. Step 10 - Add minced garlic and cook for 30-60 seconds until fragrant (do not burn). Step 11 - Pour in heavy cream. Stir to combine with the butter and garlic. Step 12 - Bring to a gentle simmer (not a rolling boil). Cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened. Step 13 - Reduce heat to low. Gradually add grated Parmesan cheese, a handful at a time, stirring constantly until melted and smooth. Step 14 - Season with salt, black pepper, and nutmeg (if using). Step 15 - If the sauce is too thick, add reserved pasta water a little at a time until desired consistency. Step 16 - Return the cooked chicken to the skillet. Stir to coat in the sauce. Step 17 - Add the drained fettuccine to the skillet. Toss gently with tongs to coat every strand of pasta in the sauce. Step 18 - If needed, add a splash more pasta water to loosen the sauce. Step 19 - Heat everything together for 1-2 minutes until warmed through. Step 20 - Garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Step 21 - Serve immediately on warm plates. Step 22 - Top with extra grated Parmesan cheese.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with garlic bread and a side Caesar salad or steamed broccoli. For a lighter version, substitute half the cream with chicken broth.
alternative_cooking: Add steamed broccoli, sun-dried tomatoes, mushrooms, or spinach to the sauce for extra vegetables. For shrimp Alfredo, substitute shrimp for chicken and cook for 2-3 minutes.

name: Tacos
description: Traditional Mexican street food of warm corn or flour tortillas filled with seasoned ground beef, fresh vegetables, cheese, and salsa.
ingredients: 500g ground beef (80/20)|1 onion (finely chopped, divided)|3 cloves garlic (minced)|2 tablespoons chili powder|1 teaspoon ground cumin|1 teaspoon paprika|1/2 teaspoon dried oregano|1/2 teaspoon salt|1/4 teaspoon black pepper|1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional)|120ml beef broth or water|12 small corn or flour tortillas|Toppings: 1 cup shredded lettuce, 1 cup diced tomatoes, 1 cup shredded cheddar or Mexican blend cheese, 1/2 cup sour cream, 1/2 cup salsa or pico de gallo, 1 avocado (sliced or guacamole), Fresh cilantro (chopped), Lime wedges
instructions: Step 1 - Make the taco seasoning: In a small bowl, mix chili powder, cumin, paprika, oregano, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper (if using). Set aside. Step 2 - Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Step 3 - Add ground beef and half of the chopped onion. Cook for 5-7 minutes, breaking up the meat with a wooden spoon, until browned and no longer pink. Step 4 - Drain excess fat from the skillet, leaving about 1 tablespoon. Step 5 - Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant. Step 6 - Sprinkle the taco seasoning over the meat. Stir to coat evenly. Step 7 - Pour in beef broth or water. Bring to a simmer. Step 8 - Reduce heat to low and cook for 5-7 minutes until most of the liquid has evaporated and the meat is coated in a thick sauce. Step 9 - While the meat cooks, warm the tortillas: Method 1 - In a dry skillet over medium heat for 30 seconds per side. Method 2 - Wrap in foil and heat in oven at 180°C (350°F) for 10 minutes. Method 3 - Directly over a gas flame using tongs for a few seconds per side (charred spots are delicious). Step 10 - Keep warm tortillas covered with a clean kitchen towel. Step 11 - Prepare all toppings: Shred lettuce, dice tomatoes, shred cheese, slice avocado, chop cilantro, and cut lime wedges. Step 12 - Assemble the tacos: Place a warm tortilla on a plate. Step 13 - Spoon about 2-3 tablespoons of seasoned beef down the center of the tortilla. Step 14 - Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheese, a dollop of sour cream, salsa, and sliced avocado. Step 15 - Sprinkle with fresh cilantro. Step 16 - Squeeze fresh lime juice over the top. Step 17 - Fold the tortilla in half or leave open (street style). Step 18 - Serve immediately. To eat: Pick up with hands. Tacos are meant to be eaten with your hands, not utensils.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with Mexican rice, refried beans, tortilla chips with salsa and guacamole on the side. Offer extra lime wedges and hot sauce for guests to customize.
alternative_cooking: For chicken tacos, use shredded cooked chicken breast tossed with taco seasoning and a little broth. For fish tacos, use battered or grilled white fish (cod or tilapia) with cabbage slaw and crema. For vegetarian tacos, substitute black beans or lentils for ground beef.

name: Chocolate Brownies
description: Rich, fudgy chocolate brownies with a crackly top and dense, moist center — a classic American dessert.
ingredients: 200g dark chocolate (60-70% cocoa), chopped|150g unsalted butter|250g granulated sugar|100g brown sugar (light or dark)|3 large eggs|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|100g all-purpose flour|30g cocoa powder (unsweetened)|1/2 teaspoon salt|Optional additions: 100g chocolate chips or chunks, 50g chopped walnuts or pecans
ingredient_names: dark chocolate|butter|granulated sugar|brown sugar|eggs|vanilla|flour|cocoa powder|salt|chocolate chips|walnuts
instructions: Step 1 - Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Step 2 - Grease a 20x20cm (8x8 inch) square baking pan. Line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on two sides for easy removal. Step 3 - In a heatproof bowl set over a pot of simmering water (double boiler), combine chopped dark chocolate and butter. Step 4 - Stir occasionally until completely melted and smooth. Remove from heat and let cool slightly for 5 minutes. Step 5 - In a large bowl, whisk together granulated sugar, brown sugar, eggs, and vanilla extract until well combined and slightly pale (about 1-2 minutes). Do not overbeat. Step 6 - Pour the melted chocolate-butter mixture into the egg-sugar mixture. Stir gently until just combined. Step 7 - In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, and salt. Step 8 - Fold the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients using a spatula. Stir until just combined and no streaks of flour remain. Do not overmix — this keeps brownies fudgy. Step 9 - Fold in chocolate chips or nuts if using. Step 10 - Pour the batter into the prepared pan. Spread evenly with a spatula. Step 11 - Bake for 25-30 minutes. The brownies are done when the top is crackly and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs (not wet batter). Step 12 - Do not overbake — brownies continue to cook as they cool. Step 13 - Let brownies cool completely in the pan on a wire rack (about 1-2 hours). Step 14 - Use the parchment overhang to lift the brownies out of the pan. Step 15 - Cut into 9 large or 16 small squares. For clean cuts, use a sharp knife wiped clean between cuts.
serving_suggestion: Serve as a dessert with vanilla ice cream, whipped cream, or a dusting of powdered sugar. Pairs well with cold milk, coffee, or hot chocolate.
alternative_cooking: For extra fudgy brownies, use only egg yolks (4 yolks instead of 3 whole eggs). For cake-like brownies, add 1/2 teaspoon baking powder. Add a swirl of peanut butter or caramel before baking for variety.

name: French Toast
description: Thick slices of bread soaked in a sweet vanilla-cinnamon egg mixture and fried until golden brown — a classic breakfast favorite.
ingredients: 8 thick slices of bread (brioche, challah, Texas toast, or day-old French bread)|3 large eggs|240ml whole milk (or half-and-half for richer flavor)|1 tablespoon granulated sugar|1 teaspoon vanilla extract|1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon|Pinch of salt|2 tablespoons unsalted butter (for cooking, plus more for serving)|For serving: Maple syrup|Powdered sugar|Fresh berries|Whipped cream
instructions: Step 1 - If using fresh bread, let slices sit out on a wire rack for 30-60 minutes to dry slightly. Day-old bread works best as it absorbs more custard without becoming soggy. Step 2 - In a shallow dish (like a pie plate or baking dish), whisk together eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt until well combined and smooth. Step 3 - Heat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Step 4 - Add 1 tablespoon of butter to the pan and let it melt and foam (but not brown). Step 5 - Working in batches, dip each slice of bread into the egg mixture for about 15-20 seconds per side. Do not soak — the bread should be coated but not saturated. Step 6 - Let excess custard drip off. Step 7 - Place the coated bread slices in the hot skillet. Do not overcrowd. Step 8 - Cook for 2-3 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crisp. Step 9 - Flip using a spatula. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the second side is golden brown and the french toast is puffed slightly. Step 10 - Transfer cooked french toast to a plate. Keep warm in a low oven (about 95°C / 200°F) while cooking remaining batches. Step 11 - Add more butter to the pan between batches as needed. Step 12 - Serve hot on individual plates. Step 13 - Top with a pat of butter. Step 14 - Dust with powdered sugar. Step 15 - Drizzle generously with maple syrup. Step 16 - Add fresh berries and whipped cream if desired.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot for breakfast or brunch with crispy bacon or breakfast sausage on the side. Also delicious with fresh fruit, honey, nutella, or a drizzle of chocolate sauce.
alternative_cooking: For stuffed french toast, make a sandwich with cream cheese or peanut butter between two slices before dipping. For baked french toast, arrange coated slices in a baking dish, cover, and refrigerate overnight, then bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes. For vegan french toast, use plant-based milk and a flax egg (1 tablespoon ground flaxseed mixed with 3 tablespoons water).

name: Omelette
description: Fluffy French-style omelette filled with cheese, herbs, and optional vegetables — a quick and satisfying breakfast or light meal.
ingredients: 3 large eggs|1 tablespoon milk or water|1 tablespoon butter|Salt to taste|Black pepper to taste|2 tablespoons grated cheese (cheddar or mozzarella)|1 tablespoon fresh chives or parsley (chopped)
instructions: Step 1 - Crack eggs into a bowl. Add milk or water, salt, and black pepper. Step 2 - Whisk vigorously with a fork or whisk until the eggs are completely combined and slightly frothy (about 30-45 seconds). Do not over-whisk. Step 3 - Heat an 8-inch non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Step 4 - Add butter and swirl the pan to coat the entire surface. When the butter stops foaming (but before it browns), proceed to the next step. Step 5 - Pour the eggs into the hot skillet. Let them sit undisturbed for 5 seconds. Step 6 - Using a spatula, gently push cooked eggs from the edges toward the center while tilting the pan to let uncooked egg flow to the edges. Step 7 - Repeat this motion for 30-45 seconds until the eggs are mostly set but still slightly soft and creamy on top. Step 8 - Sprinkle grated cheese and fresh herbs over one half of the omelette. Step 9 - Using a spatula, carefully fold the unfilled half over the filling. Step 10 - Let cook for 30 more seconds to melt the cheese. Step 11 - Slide the omelette onto a plate, folded side down. Step 12 - Garnish with additional fresh herbs if desired. Serve immediately.
serving_suggestion: Serve hot with toast, fresh fruit, sliced avocado, or a side salad. Perfect for breakfast, brunch, or a light dinner.
alternative_cooking: For a Western omelette, add 2 tablespoons each of diced ham, bell peppers, and onions — cook vegetables in the butter before adding eggs. For a mushroom and spinach omelette, sauté sliced mushrooms and fresh spinach before adding eggs. For a plain French omelette, omit cheese and herbs and cook for only 20-25 seconds total for a soft, creamy interior.
